mobileHelp.title: Getting Started on Mobile
mobileHelp.description: First steps for using cycleCAD on your phone or tablet
mobileHelp.content:
1. Navigate to cyclecad.com/app on your device
2. Allow any permission requests
3. App will download on first load (~50MB)
4. Start creating or importing designs

The app auto-saves every 30 seconds.

mobileHelp.title: Tap Gesture
mobileHelp.description: Quick touch and release for selecting and activating
mobileHelp.content:
Tap is the primary touch interaction:
• Tap 3D part: Select and show details
• Tap button: Activate function
• Tap tree item: Expand/collapse or select
• Tap viewport: Deselect current part

Tap once = single action
Tap twice quickly = double-tap

mobileHelp.title: Double-Tap Gesture
mobileHelp.description: Quick double-tap to zoom or focus
mobileHelp.content:
Double-tap functionality:
• Double-tap part: Fit part to viewport
• Double-tap empty area: Fit all parts to view
• Double-tap toolbar button: Show tool options

Double-tap is great for quickly centering your view on what you're working on.

mobileHelp.title: Long-Press (Context Menu)
mobileHelp.description: Press and hold for 500ms to open context menu
mobileHelp.content:
Long-press shows advanced options:
• Long-press part: Hide, isolate, move, delete, export
• Long-press tree item: Suppress, rename, delete
• Long-press toolbar button: Show keyboard shortcut

A haptic vibration indicates long-press registered.
Context menu appears from bottom of screen.

mobileHelp.title: Swipe Gesture
mobileHelp.description: Drag and release to navigate panels
mobileHelp.content:
Swipe opens/closes panels:
• Swipe right: Open left model tree panel
• Swipe left: Open right properties panel
• Swipe up: Expand timeline or bottom sheet
• Swipe down: Collapse timeline or close sheet

Use swipes to quickly navigate between panels.

mobileHelp.title: Pinch-to-Zoom
mobileHelp.description: Two-finger pinch gesture for zooming viewport
mobileHelp.content:
Use two fingers to zoom:
• Spread apart: Zoom in
• Pinch together: Zoom out
• Works smoothly with momentum

Can pinch on either viewport or sketch canvas.
Zoom has min/max limits to prevent going too close or far.

mobileHelp.title: Two-Finger Rotate
mobileHelp.description: Rotate viewport with two-finger twist gesture
mobileHelp.content:
Two-finger rotation:
• Place two fingers on screen
• Rotate clockwise: Rotates view clockwise
• Rotate counter-clockwise: Rotates view counter-clockwise

Independent from pinch-zoom. Can pinch and rotate simultaneously.

mobileHelp.title: Two-Finger Pan
mobileHelp.description: Move viewport without rotating
mobileHelp.content:
Pan the view with two fingers:
• Place two fingers on viewport
• Drag together in any direction
• Moves view left/right/up/down

Useful for repositioning without rotating.
Equivalent to right-click drag on desktop.

mobileHelp.title: Three-Finger Tap Undo
mobileHelp.description: Quick undo without accessing menu
mobileHelp.content:
Three-finger gesture shortcuts:
• Three-finger tap: Undo (Ctrl+Z)
• Four-finger tap: Redo (Ctrl+Y)

No menu needed - just tap with three or four fingers on viewport.
Useful for quick undo during modeling.

mobileHelp.title: Portrait Mode Layout
mobileHelp.description: Single-panel layout optimized for phone portrait
mobileHelp.content:
Portrait phone layout (<480px):

Header (44px): Menu button + title

3D Viewport: Main work area, full screen

Bottom Toolbar (44px): 6-8 most-used tools + "More" button

Swipe left/right to open side panels.
Landscape mode recommended for detailed work.

mobileHelp.title: Landscape Mode Layout
mobileHelp.description: Wider layout for phones in landscape orientation
mobileHelp.content:
Landscape phone layout (480-600px):

Header: Menu + title

Layout:
• Left sidebar (60px): Collapsed tree panel
• Center: 3D Viewport (main area)
• Right sidebar (if open): Properties panel

Bottom toolbar with tools.

Better for detailed work - use landscape for sketching.

mobileHelp.title: Tablet Layout
mobileHelp.description: Multi-column layout for tablets with more space
mobileHelp.content:
Tablet layout (600px+):

More screen space allows:
• Left panel (200px): Full model tree visible
• Center: 3D Viewport
• Right panel (280px): Properties always visible
• Top toolbar: Full buttons with labels
• Bottom: Status bar

Optimal for CAD work - tablets recommended for serious modeling.

mobileHelp.title: Left Model Tree Panel
mobileHelp.description: Navigate and manage your model's feature tree
mobileHelp.content:
Model tree shows:
• All features in order created
• Expand/collapse folder structure
• Current selected feature highlighted

Actions:
• Tap feature: Select and highlight in 3D
• Long-press: Suppress, rename, delete, show/hide
• Drag to reorder: Reorganize features

Opening: Swipe right or tap model tree icon

mobileHelp.title: Right Properties Panel
mobileHelp.description: View and edit properties of selected part or feature
mobileHelp.content:
Properties panel shows:
• Selected part/feature name
• Dimensions and parameters
• Material and appearance settings
• Constraints and relationships

Actions:
• Tap to edit values inline
• Change material/color
• Adjust dimensions with sliders
• See real-time 3D updates

Opening: Swipe left or tap properties icon

mobileHelp.title: Creating Sketches on Mobile
mobileHelp.description: Drawing 2D sketches on phone or tablet
mobileHelp.content:
Sketch workflow:
1. Tap ✏️ Sketch button in toolbar
2. Select face or plane in 3D
3. Sketch canvas appears as overlay
4. Draw with line/rectangle/circle tools
5. Add constraints for precision
6. Tap ✓ Done to finish

Tip: Use landscape mode for sketching.
Larger screen = easier drawing.

mobileHelp.title: Drawing on Sketch Canvas
mobileHelp.description: Using sketch tools to create geometry
mobileHelp.content:
Basic sketch tools:
• ✏️ Line: Tap points to draw connected lines
• ⬜ Rectangle: Tap corner 1, then corner 2
• ⭕ Circle: Tap center, then radius point
• ⌒ Arc: Tap start, arc point, then end

More tools in bottom sheet (⋯ More):
• Polyline, Bezier, Ellipse, Point, etc.

Tip: Grid snapping is automatic. Points snap to grid and endpoints.

mobileHelp.title: Constraints in Sketches
mobileHelp.description: Add dimensional and geometric constraints
mobileHelp.content:
Constraints ensure precision:
• Distance: Fixed length or radius
• Angle: Between lines
• Horizontal/Vertical: Lines aligned to axes
• Parallel/Perpendicular: Line relationships
• Equal: Same length or radius
• Tangent: Curves touching smoothly
• Fixed: Points locked in place

Add constraint:
1. Tap 📐 Constraint button
2. Select constraint type
3. Select geometry
4. Enter value if needed

mobileHelp.title: Rotating the 3D View
mobileHelp.description: Rotate model with touch gestures
mobileHelp.content:
Rotation methods:
• Single-finger drag: Smooth orbit around model
• Two-finger rotate: Free rotation around all axes
• ViewCube tap: Snap to preset orientations
• Momentum: Drag and release for coasting rotation

Tip: Single-finger drag is smoothest for general rotation.
Two-finger rotate useful when you need specific angle.

mobileHelp.title: Zooming In and Out
mobileHelp.description: Zoom the viewport with pinch gesture
mobileHelp.content:
Zoom methods:
• Pinch apart: Zoom in
• Pinch together: Zoom out
• Double-tap part: Fit to view
• Double-tap empty: Fit all to view
• Mouse wheel: If using external mouse/trackpad

Zoom limits prevent:
• Going too close (clipping inside model)
• Going too far (losing model)

Smooth with momentum scrolling.

mobileHelp.title: Panning the Viewport
mobileHelp.description: Move view without rotating
mobileHelp.content:
Pan methods:
• Two-finger drag: Move view left/right/up/down
• Hold and drag: Continuous panning

Pan is useful for:
• Repositioning without changing view angle
• Moving off-center parts into view
• Fine-tuning view after rotation

Different from single-finger drag (which rotates).

mobileHelp.title: Bottom Toolbar (Mobile)
mobileHelp.description: Quick access to most-used tools on phones
mobileHelp.content:
Bottom toolbar (44px bar at bottom):
• Shows 6-8 most frequent tools
• Icons optimized for touch
• Tap tool to activate
• Long-press tool for shortcuts

Default tools:
✏️ Sketch, 🧊 Solid, 📐 Measure, 👁️ View, ⚙️ Settings, ⋯ More

Tap ⋯ More for full tool grid in bottom sheet.

mobileHelp.title: Customizing Toolbar
mobileHelp.description: Reorder and configure which tools appear in toolbar
mobileHelp.content:
Customize toolbar in Settings:
1. Tap ⚙️ Settings
2. Select "Toolbar" tab
3. Drag tools to reorder
4. Tap + to add tools
5. Tap - to remove tools

Customization is saved locally.
Different layouts can be saved for different tasks.

mobileHelp.title: Exporting STEP Files
mobileHelp.description: Save work as STEP format for other CAD software
mobileHelp.content:
Export STEP:
1. Tap 💾 Export in toolbar
2. Select "STEP (.stp)" format
3. Choose save location (cloud, local, email)
4. Confirm export
5. File downloads in background

STEP format:
• Universal CAD format
• Preserves 3D geometry
• Usable in Fusion 360, SolidWorks, FreeCAD, etc.
• Recommended for sharing designs

mobileHelp.title: Exporting STL for 3D Printing
mobileHelp.description: Save model in STL format for 3D printing
mobileHelp.content:
Export STL:
1. Tap 💾 Export in toolbar
2. Select "STL (.stl)" format
3. Choose ASCII or Binary (binary smaller)
4. Save location
5. Send to 3D printer or slicing software

STL format:
• Standard for 3D printing
• Mesh-based (not parametric)
• Works with all slicers (Cura, PrusaSlicer, etc.)
• Can't be edited after export

mobileHelp.title: Saving Battery on Mobile
mobileHelp.description: Tips for reducing battery drain on phones
mobileHelp.content:
Battery optimization:
• Reduce graphics quality: Settings → Graphics → Low
• Disable auto-save: Settings → Auto-save → Off
• Turn off shadows: Settings → Shadows → Off
• Close other apps
• Reduce screen brightness
• Work in landscape (less frequent redraw)
• Hide unused parts

On older devices, Low quality = 40% less battery drain.

mobileHelp.title: Improving Frame Rate
mobileHelp.description: Speed up viewport rendering
mobileHelp.content:
Frame rate optimization:
• Reduce graphics quality: Settings → Low
• Disable shadows and reflections
• Hide complex parts not being edited
• Restart app if sluggish
• Close other browser tabs
• Update browser to latest version
• Reduce model detail (delete construction geometry)

Target: 30+ FPS smooth, 60 FPS ideal on tablets.

mobileHelp.title: Using Landscape Mode
mobileHelp.description: Best practices for landscape orientation
mobileHelp.content:
Landscape mode advantages:
• Wider viewport for detailed work
• More space for sidebar panels
• Better for sketching and precision
• Easier to see larger models

Recommendations:
• Use landscape for any detailed modeling
• Use portrait for quick viewing only
• Rotate device for task-specific layout
• Landscape is essential for sketching

Auto-rotation enabled by default.

mobileHelp.title: Working Offline
mobileHelp.description: Using cycleCAD without internet connection
mobileHelp.content:
Offline capabilities:
• Create and edit designs fully offline
• Auto-save works without network
• Import/export files locally
• All features available
• Work saved to device storage

Online features (require connection):
• Cloud save/sync (Pro only)
• AI features (chat, vision)
• Import from cloud storage
• Share links

Perfect for airplane, remote site, or unreliable network.

mobileHelp.title: Accessibility Features
mobileHelp.description: Making cycleCAD easier to use with disabilities
mobileHelp.content:
Accessibility options:
• Text scaling: Larger text for readability
• High contrast: Dark/light mode
• Haptic feedback: Vibration on actions
• Voice input: Say commands (with microphone)
• Screen reader support: Works with NVDA, JAWS
• Touch target size: Minimum 44x44px enforced

Accessibility settings in: Settings → Accessibility

mobileHelp.title: Touch Not Responding
mobileHelp.description: Fix unresponsive touch on mobile device
mobileHelp.content:
If touch not working:
1. Clean screen: Remove dust/oil (most common issue)
2. Restart app: Close completely and reopen
3. Hard refresh: Cmd+Shift+R (Mac) or Ctrl+Shift+R
4. Restart device: Full power cycle
5. Check for browser updates
6. Try different app area: Tap in center of viewport
7. Clear cache: Settings → Clear Cache

If still not working, post on GitHub issues.

mobileHelp.title: Import Fails
mobileHelp.description: Fix STEP or other file import errors
mobileHelp.content:
If import fails:
1. Check file size: Must be under 100MB
2. Verify format: Must be valid STEP (.stp or .step)
3. Test file: Try different STEP file
4. Clear cache: Settings → Clear Cache
5. Restart app
6. Try different browser (Chrome if using Safari)
7. Check network: Ensure stable connection

For files >100MB:
• Use desktop version of cycleCAD
• Split assembly into sub-assemblies
• Reduce part count by removing details

mobileHelp.title: Getting Help
mobileHelp.description: How to get support and report issues
mobileHelp.content:
Getting help:
• In-app help: Tap ? button or Settings → Help
• Mobile tutorial: See MOBILE-TUTORIAL.md
• GitHub issues: Report bugs at github.com/vvlars-cmd/cyclecad
• Email support: support@cyclecad.com (coming soon)
• Chat with AI: Use in-app chat for quick answers

When reporting bugs, include:
• Device model and OS version
• Browser and version
• Steps to reproduce
• Screenshot or video if possible
